reviews.review_title: This game has iffy physics that ruin it.
reviews.review_description: You cannot jump in any way limiting your movement heavily and make it hard to have fun.

developer_response.body: We will update it with better physics, but they are still not bad, and I don't think it deserves 1 star just cause of this. You can jump while on the ground and you can move while you are jumping, I don't understand what do you mean by limiting your movement.

reviews.review_description: I paid very little for this but will hopefully be able to return it. This is literally the worst game I’ve seen in the store and shouldn’t even be on app lab! Horrible graphics, the buildings are sheets so you go right through them. The score still says 0 no matter what you do. Nothing works, very glitchy, annoying voice over guy ( that you can turn off at least.) Ugly ugly graphics. How is Oculus letting this be sold for money? Not one redeeming quality to this and all positive reviews are either fake or they are brand new to VR and they’ve never played a real game before. Hope I can get my money back!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. We regret that our game did not meet your expectations. However, we'd like to address some of your concerns to provide clarity.

Firstly, we recognize that different players have varying preferences, and it's unfortunate that you found aspects of our game disappointing. Regarding the graphics, our game features a toony style designed to create an immersive experience through visual and audio effects. This stylistic choice is similar to popular games like Minecraft, Gorilla Tag, Roblox, and Rec Room, which do not rely on realistic graphics to be enjoyable.

As for the issues you mentioned, such as the buildings and score, we appreciate your detailed feedback. The game is indeed a work in progress, and we are committed to improving it. For instance, the building collision issue is known, and we are actively working on an enhanced collision system. Regarding the scoring system, it functions as intended; scores are displayed upon completing levels or reaching checkpoints.

Concerning the voice-over feature, we understand that it might not be to everyone's liking, which is why we included an option to turn it off. It's meant to enhance the game's immersive quality for those who appreciate it.

We'd also like to clarify that our reviews are genuine, coming from players who have experienced the game firsthand. Oculus ensures that reviews are legitimate by requiring players to use their own accounts and headsets.

Regarding the pricing, developing games requires significant time and resources. We offer a free demo on SideQuest for those who wish to try the game before purchasing. The paid version includes extensive content and polished features, justifying its cost.

We value constructive criticism as it helps us improve. Your feedback will be taken into consideration as we continue to develop and refine our game. If you have any further concerns or require assistance, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Thank you for your understanding and for providing us with the opportunity to improve.

reviews.review_title: Felt on edge throughout. In a good way!
reviews.review_description: Cool game really enjoying it and will look forward to playing more.
Should be free.  I know the dev like making stuff for free but this is too janky to charge money for.

developer_response.body: We understand it's not yet top quality but neither a prototype or demo to give it for free, and we charge very little. We already have one like this that is free on sidequest, called Ultra Climbing Playgrounds, this one is polished and has lots of levels, that are longer and longer. It depends what you are looking for, some don't like it because it doesn't have high graphics, but it's not that kind of game. If you are looking for a great workout while enjoying climbing through a cool toony city with nicr music and effects, then it is for you. We will update it soon and improve the mechanics a lot and other things. We are always listening to the players if they think we should improve it somehow, so please explain how would you like it to be improved? What do you mean by "janky"? Many players are happy with it and with many other games with less content and quality made by others that are paid games. We don't charge too much for this  and we are making Ultra Height 2 with HD graphics and better quality that will have a higher price.

reviews.review_description: I knew the VR and I've always had pretty bad VR sickness and even with the oculus 2 they're having games that just make me want to vomit this game does require a lot of movement but has not made me nauseous so it is perfect for those who are just beginning in VR want a good experience but get nauseous easily The movement is not nauseating unlike some games I have played and is definitely good for new players
The gam itself is a great aspect but if had a bit better physics and graphics and an actual UI woul be great and I wish I knew how to refund :(
It´s very cheap, but it has too many bugs and imo is not fun.

developer_response.body: We fixed all the bugs found by us and customers, can you please explain what bugs did you find? You can contact us at ultrafungames.io@gmail.con. And many say they had a lot of fun, but not everything works for everybody.

reviews.review_title: Who like climbing you will like this
reviews.review_description:
This game let you climb I was person that like to climb so this game is good if you like climb or bored
try this game

This game was mid to say the least. The graphics are pretty good for a two dollar game, but as it is two dollars, you can pretty much expect what you would get for two dollars I had more fun in the lobby than the actual levels.
You can climb some buildings if they are climbable and you can walk on floating platforms you can also climb ride on zip lines it is so cool and you feel really immersed

I thought it cannot be so good with these toony graphics but in the game it feels very real and really nice! I really felt like climbing a skyscraper! The game is not only a climbing game, but also a puzzle one, and it has some traps, for example in the workout mode you are not supposed to use the zipline after you scale the first building, but to go on the golden pipe and it is faster if you walk on it rather than using the hands. Alsoo when you go down those many vertical pipes, instead of grabbing them sometimes you can just jump on them if you have the balance!
It feels amazing being there and climbing! The mist looks realistic and the sound effects make it seem real. The climbing is fast and easy and you can climb up a skyscraper very fast!

This game is not just a climbimg simulator, it also has some platforming elements like moving platforms to switch between and ziplines.
The graphics is pretty basic
But it is fun, can be used for fitness, and even with the simple graphics, the fear of falling is still there.

reviews.review_title: For the price.. A no brainer
reviews.review_description: Good climbing game, smooth gameplay, lots of content, there are a few bugs in it  like being pushed off the moving platforms and sometimes not being able to grip ( think the tracking needs a tweak) and zip lines not resetting, fairly good  simple graphics and at  a price you really can't say no,, not quite as good as "to the top" though but that costs much more .. Its not perfect and needs some work but I'm sure it will improve with further updates.

reviews.review_title: This is a great budget title
reviews.review_description:
For anypone thinking that this game is just a cheap knock off of The Climb, it is not. This game incorporates other climbing elements as well. There are zip lines and moving platforms to contend with as well. There is an endless workout mode available in addition to the main levels. Additionally, there is a practice area in the main menu lobby. The game has a great amount of content for the price. It is not as polished as The Climb but it is also much much cheaper.  I have a few QOL suggestions that would bring this game to 5 stars. Even if the game remains unchanged, you cannot go wrong at this price for the amount of content you receive.

Edit: After spending some more time with the game, I have decided to change my review to 5 stars. Some of the issues I was having in the game were due to my lack of understanding. That being said, there are still some tweaks that could be made to improve the game, but I am certain they will happen in an update from community feedback. At this point, I am having a blast with the game and that’s what’s important.
The movement is bad the levels are bad I can run and the voice acting is annoying

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. We appreciate all feedback as it helps us enhance the gaming experience for everyone.

Regarding the movement, we understand that it might not be to everyone's preference, and we are continuously working on improvements based on player feedback. If you have specific suggestions on how we can enhance this aspect, we would love to hear them.

As for the levels, our goal is to create a challenging and engaging experience. We are always open to constructive criticism, so if you have specific points about what didn't work for you, please let us know so we can address them in future updates.

We recognize that voice acting is a subjective element and may not appeal to everyone. This is why we have included an option to turn it off, ensuring that players can tailor the experience to their liking.

We are committed to making our game the best it can be and appreciate your input. If there are any other aspects of the game you'd like to discuss or suggestions you have, please feel free to reach out.

reviews.review_description:
All of the other positive reviews sure hyped me up for this title, but there were just so many strange issues that I can't understand them.

Firstly, the settings at the start of any level are on a long pole, and you're at the bottom; the music options are high up on the pole, out of your reach. There is no explanation stating that you have to awkwardly climb the side of the pole while avoiding contact with the lower buttons if you want to reach the music-changing buttons at the top of the pole. Why this design in the first place?!

Secondly, the beginning tutorial levels are so needlessly focused on correctly falling onto moving platforms between skyscrapers that they ruined my expectations of the actual climbing to experience. I think it was the fourth level or so that had three moving platforms. The lowest one on the right never actually reaches the other skyscraper, nor is there any way for you to return back to your starting skyscraper once you fall onto it. The ONLY way to undo your mistake is to literally fall and kill yourself to reset the level. Really? This was the last straw and was what got me to quit the levels. Just how bad can level design get?

So I moved onto the infinite "workout" level, but there was nothing to do except to try to scale the flat, direct wall of a skyscraper up. (If I was not supposed to do that, then there was a total lack of instructions about where you ARE supposed to go. I saw a Pokéball-like thing suspended in mid-air but couldn't seem to interact with it.) The problem is that you end up hitting a ceiling as you scale the skyscraper, which you can attempt to grip and keep moving horizontally off of, but the game's collision detection is so bad that you can end up accidentally clipping through the ceiling and becoming disoriented over where your hands actually are. I tried this at least 3 times and every time my in-game hands couldn't seem to get a grip on the ceiling and eventually led to me falling every time. Terrible game.

developer_response.body:
THIS IS A TROLL! IT DOESN'T SAY ANYTHING RIGHT ABOUT THE ACTUAL GAME!

Sorry but you are whether a troll or a really bad gamer! The levels are designed perfectly well and there isn't necessarily only one way of completing a level and you, as a gamer, need to use your brain and skills to figure out the best one! The level 4 is very simple, let me explain to you how you could use the platform from the right: you go on to the top platform,  then you jump or fall on the platform on the right you are talking about, and IT IS going back to the first skyscraper, on a lower level, you don't need to jump and kill yourself! Then while you are on the lower level, you go to the lower platform on the left and you go to the second skyscraper! Duh! 
And there is a faster way of completing the level, you just jump on to the left platform while you are on the top one! You can see in this video how I completed this level in 15 seconds! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k4R3HS1fJC0

About "the settings at the start of any level are on a long pole"... it is a climbing game, not a lazy one! It's design is to match the theme! But there are also settings in the starting menu scene and they are on a desk!

About the infinite workout level: you need to climb up at first, there is a big text that says that! Then you need to follow the checkpoints that are clearly highlighted and the first ones are very easy to reach! And as I said,  it is also a game where you need to use a brain and figure out how to complete a level! And it is easy for any usual gamer, it's not like in the escape room games! You also need to use your brain a bit on how to climb an whole building and it's really not that hard!
It's a great game, you are just a terrible gamer or a troll!
And really, after all you think it deserves only one star?! Clearly a troll!

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this game, nice practice area to get you used to the controls before jumping in and making your way across the first building. 

I have a fear of heights and this game definitely gets your knees wobbling which is a great way to tell how high you feel. 

Excellently responsive climbing action and good workout.

reviews.review_title: A fun game actually scary when you fall
reviews.review_description: It's a really fun basic experience. You climb tall buildings and hope to God that you don't fall lol I almost crapped myself the fist few times I fell off. But all in all it's really fun to just climb up and be able to look around from that high up

Since this is my first climbing experience in VR, I am not able to compare it to any other VR experience.

But since I used to like climbing, but could not do this for a long time due to my physical handicap, the experience allows me to be physically active again.
After the first hour I sweated more than in any other VR experience and I was finally able to feel the high altitude again.

The main menu looks simple, but has a lot of surprises in store, you just have to climb around a little ^^

After climbing my first building, I just wanted more and worked my way up to the first high-rise. There was also now an update in which the wishes of the users were taken into account, which shows that the developers value the feedback from the players and put a lot of heart and soul into their work.

The graphics seem quite simple, but that's what I think makes the game so charming.
The hand tracking was just a little weird, but I found it more funny than annoying.

Especially if you have a disability that does not allow you to stand for a long time, it is good for you that you can continue comfortably from the swivel chair (normal chair is not recommended because it is difficult to turn on it)

I'm looking forward to the further updates and features and see how much weight I will lose with the app.

Greetings from Germany and a really big thank you to the developers,
your Peppo
